channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hypertension Affects Young Africans Due to Lifestyle Changes
item.description: Hypertension, once mainly a problem for older adults, is now increasingly affecting younger Africans. This shift is due to lifestyle changes, urban migration, and dietary shifts. Young people moving t...

item.title: The AI Winter: How Poor Machine Translation Led to a Tech Setback
item.description: The article explores the historical context of the first AI winter, a period of reduced funding and interest in artificial intelligence research. It traces the origins of this decline to the 1960s, wh...

item.title: Ex-partner murder trial begins in Melfort, Saskatchewan
item.description: A man accused of murdering his ex-partner is facing trial in Melfort, Saskatchewan, following a case that highlights the dangers of domestic violence and coercive control. The trial, which includes au...

item.title: Canadian government closes corporate watchdog despite ongoing complaints
item.description: The Canadian Ombudsperson for Responsible Enterprise (CORE), a watchdog established in 2019 to investigate potential human rights abuses by Canadian companies abroad, was abruptly dissolved by Prime M...

item.title: Kajiado Rehab Centre Closed Over Child Abuse and Illegal Detention
item.description: Authorities in Kenya have ordered the immediate closure of the Ehsan Rehabilitation Centre following the rescue of a 14-year-old boy and the discovery of alleged child abuse, physical assault, illegal...

item.title: Global Oil Prices Dip Slightly Amid Market Volatility
item.description: Oil prices experienced a minor decline on Wednesday as market participants adjusted to recent geopolitical developments and evolving economic data. The benchmark Brent crude and WTI crude futures both...

item.title: Benhur Abalos to represent Marcos Jr. as Cabinet secretary in Philippine tour
item.description: Benhur Abalos, a key figure in the Philippine government, is set to embark on a nationwide tour to represent Ferdinand Marcos Jr. as the proposed Cabinet secretary. The announcement by the Palace high...

item.title: Foo Fighters Deliver a High-Energy Performance at Mad Cool Festival, Reflecting on 30 Years of Music
item.description: Dave Grohl and Foo Fighters delivered a high-energy performance at the Mad Cool Festival in Madrid, marking the band's return to the venue after a decade. The concert, held on July 8, 2026, featured a...

item.title: Controversy over PFIPC budget allocation as officials clash over legitimacy
item.description: The news article details a heated political controversy surrounding the alleged budget allocation of a non-existent agency called PFIPC. The purported Director General (DG) of the agency, Adeyemi, cla...

item.title: Pakistan Navy Successfully Rescues 20 Crew Members from Sinking Dhow Near Ormara
item.description: The Pakistan Navy (PN) and Pakistan Maritime Security Agency (PMSA) executed a swift rescue operation to save 20 crew members from a sinking cargo dhow located east of Ormara. The vessel was experienc...

item.title: Boko Haram attacks military base and police post in Borno, killing soldier
item.description: Suspected Boko Haram terrorists launched a coordinated attack on Logomani community in Ngala Local Government Area of Borno State, targeting a military base and a police outpost. The assault, which al...

item.title: Trump's Law Cuts Aid for 98K Pennsylvanians Ahead of Key Election
item.description: President Donald Trump's sweeping spending and tax legislation, known as the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' has led to approximately 98,000 Pennsylvanians losing access to essential food assistance pro...

item.title: Australia and India finalize uranium export agreement for peaceful use
item.description: Australia and India have resolved long-standing administrative hurdles to commence uranium exports for peaceful purposes, marking a significant step in their bilateral energy cooperation. The agreemen...

item.title: Spaza shops in Soweto resume operations as community relies on informal trade
item.description: Spaza shops in Soweto have resumed operations following anti-migrant protests, with owners returning to their businesses after initially closing to prevent looting. Shopkeepers like Selamu Masebo, an ...

item.title: Tanzania's Agricultural Transformation Focuses on Market Strength and Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania is prioritizing market development as a cornerstone for its agricultural transformation, moving beyond mere production to create efficient systems that drive economic growth. The article high...

item.title: Philippines Defends Sovereignty Over Batanes Amid Claims by China
item.description: The Philippines has firmly rejected allegations that the northern province of Batanes belongs to China, asserting that its sovereignty is settled and not open to discussion. In a statement released by...

item.title: Doctor Celebrates First Car Purchase After Years of Hard Work in Australia
item.description: Dr Fayth, a female doctor based in Australia, celebrated buying her very first car, a Porsche Macan S Twin-Turbocharged, after years of grinding through the demands of medicine. The milestone was shar...

item.title: The Role of Communication in Strengthening Marital Relationships
item.description: Effective communication is vital for maintaining healthy marriages, as it fosters love, trust, and understanding between partners. Many couples overlook the importance of meaningful dialogue, but expe...

item.title: Fremantle and Sydney Swans clash in crucial AFL 2026 match
item.description: The AFL 2026 season reached a pivotal moment as Fremantle, the league leaders, host the second-placed Sydney Swans in a high-stakes encounter in Perth. The match, set against a backdrop of intense riv...

item.title: DepEd addresses surge in school violence following Tacloban shooting incident
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has reported a significant increase in school-related violence incidents across the Philippines, citing the deadly Tacloban shooting as a catalyst for the 'copycat'...
